description:
Those long tousled locks with the swept-out fringe, by way of Daisy Edgar-Jones, culminate into one of this past season’swidely sought afteryet effortless cool-girl cuts. With the face-framing bangs, worn loose or blown out for that old-school glam look, it remainsfirmly on trend for 2026.
Edgar-Jones has a softly, softly approach to her hair, moving assuredly and slowly over the years from a jaw-length bob to ’70s-leaning curtain bangs. Same goes for her hair color: it’s been a chocolatey, espresso shade, painted with caramel highlights, and worked up to abeachy blondelob. It’s coloristJacob Schwartz(called upon by everyone fromBella Hadidto Margot Robbie) who’s beenalong for the ride and every subtle hair transformation.
A chocolatey shade for Daisy Edgar-Jones.
Some lighter, caramel layers.
This latest look falls in total tandem with her fashions: DEJ loves ’70s-tipped style, textures, and sumptuous color palettes. Think beige suede jackets paired with Gucci cowboy boots, a confectionerybrown Chloé leather, or this latest look: a diaphanous Valentino organza top with an amber floral fastening.
Make like DEJ this season and consider this blushing brown shade when you’re next in the chair: unlike most red shades, it’s pretty easy to maintain and grow out, and instantly elevates your bob or romantic long layers. Strawberry brunette season is here!

trend_name: Jonathan Anderson’s Dior Celebrates Parisian and London Girl Beauty
description:
All products featured on Vogue are independently selected by our editors. However, we may receive compensation from retailers and/or from purchases of products through these links.
This afternoon in Paris’s Tuileries Garden, Jonathan Anderson’sDior Fall/Winter 2026 outdoor runwaywas sunny, sweaty, and full of VIPs. Ushers in standing collar jackets rushed silver trays stacked with cold bottles of Evian to hot attendees sitting under a plexiglass roof. In the front row, Pharrell sat next to Anna Wintour, fluttering his invitation like a makeshift fan. After reuniting withLove Storycastmate Dree Hemingway, Paul Anthony Kelly could be seen bopping to the beats of Frédéric Sanchez’s soundtrack as models walked in jeans embellished with sparkling bunting, fluttering skirts with glittering hems, and lilypad heels that matched the faux waterlilies floating in the fountain under the catwalk. “Les Tuileries has a whole history as a park created to parade—to see and be seen,” makeup artist Peter Philips toldVoguebackstage.
Over the centuries since it was built in 1564, people have used the park for casual meetings, “la rencontre,” Philips, who is creative and image director of Christian Dior makeup, says.“It could be something romantic, could be something business, could be something illegal,” he explains with eyebrows raised. “There's a bit of a dangerous twist.” During Sunday’s discussions with Anderson, a twist onla rencontreinspired the beauty look: “It never was part of the briefing, but bizarrely, in the conversation there was something really funny that happened. He’s like, ‘Oh, I just want something fresh, a little bit like a Parisian girl when they have something like a leftover kohl or leftover mascara, like a bit messy on the eye, but very subtle,’” says Philips of Anderson’s direction. Charlotte Gainsbourg, the French-British actor and singer, came up as they conceptualized the beauty with hair artist Guido Palau. “They start talking about maybe the hair is something like a London girl, a bit messy, a bit Kate Moss-y,” says Philips. “And I was like, ‘Oh, this is likela rencontre—a Parisian girl and a London girl, mixed into one."
And so today, the creative and image director of Christian Dior makeup is prepping models for this “messy leftover makeup look” steeped into the house's beauty codes, which “capture youth and a little playfulness.” Cute logo-edDior Les Patchs Yeuxwere applied under models’ eyes before he sent them to Palau’s team for low ponytails with side bangs and plenty of wisps. “Jonathan wanted to reference a sort of ease, but have a little style,” Palau explains. His team of hairstylists around us can be seen trimming extensions for the piecey flyaways pushed in front of the ears. “Everything's soft. It's like a cool French girl.” Palau’s low ponies were all overVogue’s Milan Trend Report, too, and he says, “this isn’t the end of the ponytail,” and soon we’ll see even more. “You can make them glamorous, rough, you can give attitude.” For Anderson’s Dior, though, “this is a French girl to me,” says Palau. “You never feel that anything's tooworked.”
Nail artist Ama Quashie recreated her clean manicure that inspired Anderson to request “just a buff” last season. Again, it was achieved with a sheer coat ofDior Base VernisandDior Nail Glow. Philips kept things just as chill. “No blush, no contour, no color on the face,” he said, simply brushing on his new skincare-infusedDior Forever Skin Glow Foundation. “It's a different kind of glow.” On the eyes, the look was slept-in. After applyingDiorshow On Stage Crayon in 099 Blackto models’ top waterline, “I ask them to look down, and I say,okay now squeeze,” he says, squinting eyes closed. It’s a trick that transfers color without too much fuss. “It has to stay light and natural,” he simply curls bare lashes and shapes natural arches with DiorshowOn Set BrowandBrow Styler.
As a final touch, “I use theLip Maximizer in 001 Pinkto hydrate and give a peppery, tickly, plumping sensation,” Philips says. “Because JW doesn't like a shiny lip or anything,” so it’s better to remove any glossy texture rather than guild the lily. It meant Philips was working right up to the moment models stepped onto the runway: “Just before they go on, I'll tap it down so the shine is gone.”

Do you like to wear your long hair down? You have options. Why not elevate your look with some side-swept styling, floral accessories, or a sparkling headpiece? Are you all about DIY? We have just the inspiration for you.
Related Video
Ariana Grande’s ’60s Cat Eye and “Pretty In Pink” Makeup Look
From Diana Ross’s baby’s breath-adorned chignon toJane Birkin’s untamed floral crown and Demi Lovato’s glamorous waves, here are some of the best celebrity wedding hairstyles to ever make their way down the aisle.

Barefoot and blissful, Crawford's laissez-faire loose waves complement her casual beachside wedding. Get the look by loosely braiding towel-dried lengths, allowing to air dry, and finishing with your favorite sea spray.

For a whimsical, regal twist, Margherita Missoni accentuates her classic braidedupdowith a glintingsilver tiara. The accessory elevates the milkmaid braid to something more glamorous—and shouldn't your wedding day find you feeling like romantic royalty?
Sweet and simple is the name of the game for this Dutch stunner. Bette Frank's braid crown is made stronger for its easy weave, the loose French furthered by romantic flyaways and natural texture.

Ingrid Bergman's pin curls peek out from her Juliet cap in this vintage bridal style. Though this style may have been more of a standard during Bergman's 1937 nuptials, an above-neck revival offers the perfect amount of era-inspired bridal beauty.

Flower crowns can come with a certain amount of cheese, but a well-executed floral accessory is elegant, playful, and romantic. Audrey Hepburn's dark hair provides a striking backdrop for a stunning crown of white rosettes.

The ultimate wedding hairstyles are a slight amplification of the bride's preferred aesthetic—and for Priscilla Presley, that translates to a stylized bouffant. In case you didn't know this was anoccasion, a rhinestone crown and three-foot veil top off the gravity-defying style.
Sometimes, wedding day glamour is best achieved through understated elegance. Here, Mia Farrow is the poster child of gamine chic with her shortpixie cutand simple white skirt suit.

The year was 1952, and Jacqueline Kennedy’s hair was pinned in an elegant updo as she said “I do” in front of 700 guests. To update Kennedy's coiffure, keep the curls and sweetly pinned approach, adapting the silhouette to suit your style.
Disco meets flower child in the late-70s style. Marisa Berenson frames her iconic natural curls with a handful of delicate white irises for her walk down a Beverly Hills aisle.

Leave it to Grace Kelly, Princess of Monaco, to deliver one of the best wedding hairstyles of all time. The actress-turned-princess accented her taut, polished, confection of a twist with a long, lace veil to create a moment worthy of a royal wedding.
Farah Fawcett's signature feathered waves made an extra-teased appearance at her 1970s wedding in Los Angeles, a sun hat topper making the style all the more summery, glamorous, and bohemian.

A sleek low know is chic and timeless, all while providing the ideal foundation for a collection of bridal accessories. Here, white flowers and a veil accent Diana Ross's raven, slicked-back wedding hair.

Come-as-you-are beauty is a surefire means of ensuring that you feel like yourself on your big day. Rather than an overwrought updo, Brigitte Bardot opted for her easy bombshell blonde for her wedding to actor Jacques Charrier.
Lauren Bacall's deep part and polished waves epitomize Old Hollywood glamour. Retro hair is back in a big way, making the present the perfect time to recreate this soft and sophisticated style.
Not one to stray from what works, Marilyn Monroe wears her bridal bombshell blonde chop pinned up into classically romantic curls, her coif covered with a chin-length wedding veil.
Bianca Jagger's bridal mane is worthy of a rockstar wedding. The party girl's hair fell in a styled cascade of brushed curls, her brunette mane peeking from beneath her Yves Saint Laurent chapeau.
There's something to be said for simply wearing what works on your wedding day. Betty Catroux's wedding hair is identical to her everyday hair: bohemian, pin straight, and heavily banged.
Why let your bouquet have all the floral fun? Socialite Minnie Cushing wears her hair is pulled up off her face, teased into a voluminous beehive, and adorned with a sweet smattering of daisies.
Yoko Ono's white felt hat is the ideal topper for her freewheeling mane, worn for her wedding to John Lennon near Gibraltar, Spain. Here, air-dried texture and a want of Mod are the styling stars.
This slicked updo is forever. Vanessa Traina's picture-perfect bun is pinned just above the nape of the neck, covered (and elevated) by her cascading (and custom-made) Givenchy tulle bridal veil.
Jane Birkin's bangs are iconic and render any superfluous additions entirely unnecessary—even on her wedding day. A loose lob and subtly flipped out ends make for jaunty, celebratory wedding hair.

The early ‘80s demanded drama, as demonstrated by this piled-high style worn by modern Pat Cleveland. This textural updo functions as an elegant addition while keeping the hair out of Cleveland’s face.

A pinned, slapdash updo gets a surrealist addition by way of an unexpected addition. A rose in full bloom is tucked into the Nouvelle Vague's Anna Karina's banged and curled style, the floral accessory making a major, surprisingly funky statement.
Caroline Trentini's textured, raked-back chignon is effortlessly chic. With a few loose pieces of hair framing her face and a low-positioned veil, the style feels timeless, Old World, and relevant.
Jemma Kidd, the Countess of Mornington, opts for a classic, center-separated bun. Though a standard for the style set, the sleek option doubles as a wedding hairstyle fit for English nobility.
Delicate florals are tucked into Zosia Mamet's easy half-up style for a touch of appropriately bohemian whimsy. Loose face-framing bangs lend to the wedding hair's effortless nature.
A simple low-slung style can make a major visual impact. Serena Williams's amber-tinted lengths are woven into a generous nape-of-the-neck knot, the twist positioned to one side to romantic effect.
Actor Nazanin Mandi's updo hinges on just the right amount of tease at the crown and a set of side-swept tendrils, making the case for a modernized bouffant. When in doubt, opt for ease and volume.

Hailey Bieber is one for a pared-down trademark look—and when it comes to her hair, that look is a slicked, center-parted knot. As such, it's unsurprising that she selected the same style (though veil-topped) for her wedding day.

With its deep side part and a panel of loose waves, Paris Hilton's platinum updo showcases the beauty of little asymmetry. Plus. the perfectly polished finished is in keeping with Hilton's ever-perfect styling.

Nicola Peltz wears a half-up with wispy curtain bangs, the look inspired by a 1990s-era Claudia Schiffer. Brunette roots and the bohemian bang team to make the classically romantic wedding hair feel cool and of-the-moment.

True to her penchant for all things chic, understated, and relaxed, Alexandra Daddario's scraped-back knot is both practical and elegant. A deep side part adds the perfect amount of elegant drama to the otherwise simple style.

Priyanka Chopra and Nick Jonas celebrated their nuptials across over a series of celebrations. For this ceremony in New Delhi, fresh florals adorned both the sides of Chopra's updo. Fragrant and fanciful.
Beanie Feldstein's summer camp-themed wedding weekend had tons of personal and gorgeous touches—and for the welcome party she greeted guests in this silken floral dress with a matching headband.
In custom Chanel
Hairstylist Serge Normant created a classic modern loose chignon for Meghan Markle's wedding day.
Taylor Hill's multi-day wedding came with a variety of styles, but the ceremony saw her brunette left loose and romantic, pinned gentle at the sides to lift lengths off of her face.
For her Portofino, Italy, wedding Kourtney Kardashian selected a veil-bedecked updo defined by tightly swept back hair so you could focus on the joy in her face.
America's Olympian sweetheart Simone Biles's bridal beauty look included mermaid waves, made with a large barrel curling iron.
Joey King's romantic and tousled blowout is made stronger through the subtle half-up styling. Rather than a full-tilt partial updo, King's pin-up comes from two small sections secured at back.
The quiet luxury trend can be partially chalked up to Sofia Richie’s nuptials, for which she wore a chic, raked-back midi knot—an ideal blend of sleek and textural.

While Barbara Palvin’s wedding day featured a series of styles, the main event took the form of polished lengths left to swing free save for a set ofembellished barrettesslid elvishly behind each year.

Dramatic flair doesn’t require deviating from your signature style. Though Kat Dennings stayed true to her signature easy waves, the addition of a floral headpiece took the look to a more romantic realm.

This content can also be viewed on the site itoriginatesfrom.
A pinned updo may be elegant and timeless, but Millie Bobby Brown’s interpretation includes a contemporary twist. A center part and quartet of face-framing tendrils make the style youthful, modern, and slightly more effortless.
For Rita Ora, blonde curls worn soft and loose are made wedding- (and party-) ready with a solitary bedazzled barette secured at one side.

The slicked knot is a 2020s cool-girl calling card, making it a natural fit for wedding wear. Olivia Culpo elevates that style with a deep side part, the rehearsal-ready updo offering an updated iteration of the classic chignon.

Rebel Wilson’s side-swept tumble of soft, waist-reaching curls has the air of a Disney princess. The cascading mane, thrown over one shoulder and supplemented by a near peekaboo bang, is balanced by a sleek section secured by an embellished barrette.

Nothing says chic quite like a ballet bun. Alex Cooper’s ’90s minimalist bridal style called for the twist-up to match, her understated blonde midi bun serving as a foundation for a down-the-back veil.

Singer-songwriter Carly Rae Jepsen went for romantic, cascading waves for her wedding at the Hotel Chelsea. Perfect for her long hair and face-framing curtain bangs, the hairstyle came together with boughs of baby’s breath delicately pinned down, creating a bohemian, natural look.

For her wedding, the actor Marisa Abela was inspired by a headshot of Audrey Hepburn fromFunny Face,wearing a diamond necklace in her hair in the style of a tiara. The result is a delicate and regal bun that lets her beauty shine.

Demi Lovato went for an Old Hollywood-style side-swept hairstyle for her wedding, making her Vivienne Westwood gown shine. For brides with long hair, this is the perfect way to wear your hair down in an elevated way.

trend_name: The Day Before Her Wedding, Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy Considered a Major Beauty Change
description: They say never make a big beauty change in the months leading up to your wedding. Chopping your hair, trying a new skin-care routine, or making major color changes are verboten. But apparently, Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy almost defied that proverb the very day before her incredibly high-profile nuptials to John F. Kennedy Jr.
